patch.value: Test-suite dedup & best-practice audit (post module-mirror reorg)

READ-ONLY audit of tests/unit (216 spec files, 4236 tests, 100/100/100/100 coverage) after the module-mirror reorg. Parent epic: pm-xau3. Goal was finding (1) cross-file duplicate test cases and (2) best-practice deviations. NO EDITS made here; lead agent must re-verify 100% coverage holds after any merge (a dedup must not drop a uniquely-covered branch).

== CONFIRMED DUPLICATE CLUSTERS (ranked by impact) ==

[D1 HIGH] background-refresh module tested twice. tests/unit/core/search/background-refresh.spec.ts (dedicated module spec) vs tests/unit/core/search/search-cache.spec.ts lines ~1712-1946. Both import the SAME src/core/search/background-refresh.js. search-cache re-tests shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground, queue enqueue/drain, and runSemanticRefreshWorker that background-refresh.spec.ts already owns. Safe merge: delete the duplicated describe blocks from search-cache.spec.ts (lines ~1712-1946) AFTER porting the few search-cache-UNIQUE cases into background-refresh.spec.ts. See comment for exact unique-case lists. ~200 line reduction.

[D2 MEDIUM] check-secrets line-numbering + include/exclude rules tested twice. tests/unit/scripts/check-secrets-rules.spec.ts vs tests/unit/scripts/check-secrets-lib.spec.ts. Both exercise scanContent line numbers + includeFiles/excludeFiles. See comment for the overlap/unique split.

No other CONFIRMED cross-file duplicates. Commands/cli, extensions/mcp/sdk/scripts agents found only LEGITIMATE separation (parser-vs-e2e, unit-vs-integration, per-command tracker-not-initialized smoke tests). The shared "fails when tracker is not initialized" test name across ~27 command specs is correct (each tests a different command), NOT a duplicate.

== BEST-PRACTICE FINDINGS (ranked) ==

1. [MED] Brittle real setTimeout/sleep instead of vi.useFakeTimers(): extension-loader.spec.ts lines ~5030/5043/5067 (deactivate-timeout tests, 25-35ms real waits, CI-flaky); telemetry-runtime.spec.ts lines ~51/1224/1678; item-store.spec.ts ~469/489; embedding-batches.spec.ts ~243. Short waits so low-flake-risk but non-deterministic.
2. [MED] Monolithic covers-everything tests in telemetry-runtime.spec.ts (e.g. lines ~1423, ~1765) with 50+ assertions each; hard to localize a failure. Consider splitting by branch area (no coverage change).
3. [LOW] Verbose inline env save/restore in telemetry-runtime.spec.ts afterEach (~lines 63-107). A withCleanEnv() helper already exists in background-refresh.spec.ts (~86-111); adopt it.
4. [LOW] extension-loader.spec.ts (5656 lines, 106 tests) under-nested: many describe blocks hold 20+ flat tests; add nested describes for readability.
5. [LOW] Real git spawnSync helper in extension-command.spec.ts (~line 81) depends on system git; consider mocking unless integration is intentional.

== POSITIVES ==
Good isolation overall: temp-dir scoping via withTempDir/withTempPmPath, mocked fetch (no real network), spies for console, try/finally restore of globals, no module-level orphan it().

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
[D1 HIGH] background-refresh duplicate — exact safe-merge map. Both files import src/core/search/background-refresh.js.

DUPLICATED (identical code path + assertions), present in BOTH:
- shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground: bg-refresh 114/119/131/138/153 vs search-cache 1717/1722/1753 (inline-under-vitest default; explicit inline override; worker-child marker).
- queue enqueue/drain: bg-refresh "merges/dedupes/sorts then drains" 162, "ignores empty/blank" 171, "malformed json / non-array" 179, "removes stale gate" 195 vs search-cache 1760/1773/1781/1790/1809.
- runSemanticRefreshWorker: bg-refresh "drains+refreshes under lock" 300, "settings cannot be read warning" 286, "re-enqueues+warns on throw" 341, "returns without draining when lock held" 352, "returns early when pm root missing" 276 vs search-cache 1848/1908/1927/1891/1939.

UNIQUE to search-cache.spec.ts (MUST be ported into background-refresh.spec.ts before deletion, or coverage drops):
- "falls back to an unguarded queue merge when the pending queue gate stays contended" (1797)
- "keeps awaited queue-gate backoff timers referenced so CLI top-level await can settle" (1822)
- "processes ids enqueued mid-refresh in a follow-up round" (1864)
NOTE: bg-refresh has near-equivalents (1797~bg 207 unguarded-merge; verify branch identity before assuming redundant). 1822 and 1864 appear genuinely unique — port them.

UNIQUE to background-refresh.spec.ts (keep): "uses default author when PM_AUTHOR unset" 318, "skips while fresh gate held by another writer" 229, "re-dispatches child on sibling enqueue in final window" 369, "does not re-dispatch after failure" 403, dispatchRefreshChild spawn tests 244/264.

Safe merge: port the 2-3 unique search-cache cases above into background-refresh.spec.ts, then delete the shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground/queue/runSemanticRefreshWorker describe blocks (search-cache ~1712-1946). Re-run coverage; background-refresh.ts must stay 100%.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
[D2 MEDIUM] check-secrets duplicate — exact safe-merge map. Both exercise scripts/check-secrets-lib.mjs scanContent.

check-secrets-rules.spec.ts imports the module ONCE at top level (shared RULES, push/pop in try/finally). check-secrets-lib.spec.ts uses createScriptHarness().importModule for a FRESH cache-busted module per test (cleaner isolation — preferred base).

DUPLICATED assertions:
- one-based line numbers: rules "reports one-based line numbers" (line 54, asserts line===3) vs lib "computes one-based line numbers across multiple newlines" (16, line===5) AND "line number of 1 for first line" (24). Same code path; lib covers more positions.
- includeFiles scoping: rules "honors includeFiles when custom rules are scoped" (39) vs lib "skips a rule whose includeFiles pattern does not match" (31). Same branch.
- excludeFiles scoping: rules "honors excludeFiles for path/host heuristics" (34) vs lib "skips a rule whose excludeFiles pattern matches" (43). Same branch (absolute-home-path under tests/).

UNIQUE to check-secrets-rules.spec.ts (MUST keep — these are the only places hit):
- "flags every GitHub token prefix" (11): ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_ + github_pat_ — github-token rule variants.
- "flags other high-risk credential shapes" (19): private-key, aws-access-key, sentry-org-token, private-ssh-target, private-ip.
- "does not flag clean documentation content" (30): zero-findings path.
- "exposes a non-empty rule set" (60): RULES array shape.

Safe merge: fold the include/exclude + line-number cases into check-secrets-lib.spec.ts (better per-test fresh-module isolation), keep the rule-coverage cases (GitHub prefixes, credential shapes, clean-doc, rule-set shape) — they are NOT in the lib spec and likely cover distinct regex branches. Re-run coverage on check-secrets-lib.mjs.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: [Best-practice cluster] Fake-timers candidates (no coverage change, removes CI flake + speeds suite): replace real setTimeout/sleep with vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync in: extension-loader.spec.ts ~5030/5043/5067 (deactivate-timeout, 25-35ms real waits + manual process.on(unhandledRejection)); telemetry-runtime.spec.ts ~51/1224/1678; item-store.spec.ts ~469/489 (fs cache poll); embedding-batches.spec.ts ~243. These are the only places real wall-clock waits appear in tests/unit — everything else is mocked. Verify each is a deterministic timeout-branch test before swapping (a couple poll real fs mtime and may need a different approach).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Dedup D1+D2 done (suite green: 215 files / 4218 tests, 0 failures via run-tests.mjs test). D1 (search): background-refresh.spec.ts now OWNS all background-refresh tests. Ported 3 genuinely-unique cases from search-cache.spec.ts: 'keeps awaited queue-gate backoff timers referenced' (setTimeout unref assertion), 'processes ids enqueued mid-refresh in a follow-up round' (rounds===2 loop-back), 'returns immediately when the queue is empty' (rounds===0 empty-drain). Deleted the duplicated 'search background refresh' module blocks from search-cache.spec.ts (~235 lines: lock-id, shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground, pending queue, runSemanticRefreshWorker) and pruned now-unused imports (REINDEX_LOCK_ID/runSemanticRefreshWorker/shouldRunSearchRefreshInForeground/acquireLock); kept drainPendingRefreshIds/enqueuePendingRefreshIds (still used by entrypoint tests) and the cache/entrypoint/isSemanticRefreshActive/searchCacheTestOnly tests that remained inside that describe. Did NOT port search-cache's unguarded-merge test (line 84 already covered by bg spec's mkdirSync-throw test). D2 (check-secrets): kept check-secrets-lib.spec.ts as the single file (createScriptHarness fresh-module isolation). Ported 4 unique tests adapted to harness.importModule: github-token prefix coverage, high-risk credential shapes (private-key/aws/sentry/ssh-target/private-ip, runtime-assembled fixtures incl split 192.168.x.x to avoid self-scan), clean-doc zero-findings, non-empty rule set. Deleted check-secrets-rules.spec.ts entirely (64 lines; its one-based-line/include/exclude tests already exist in lib spec). Coverage of check-secrets-lib.mjs + background-refresh.ts preserved by surviving tests.

patch.value: Dedup applied: D1 background-refresh + D2 check-secrets merged; 18 redundant tests removed; coverage re-verified 100/100/100/100.
